meta.description: RAY — established-protocol but does NOT satisfy is_established_protocol() per token_status.rs (jup_verified=false, score=56, not jup_strict). Signal A may fire on RAY MM activity. This is a documented calibration gap identical to D02/D04 RAY FP debt. Fixture serves to document the gap, not assert a passing test.

token.is_established_protocol_reason: jup_verified=false AND jup_strict=false — neither branch of is_established_protocol() satisfied. score=56 above threshold 40. RAY is a known FP for structural detectors (D02 Signal B, D04 Signal C) — same debt applies to D05 Signal A.

trading_pattern_summary.note: Raydium's own market maker wallet may produce H1-like round-trips on RAY. If Signal A fires on RAY, it is a false positive stemming from the same is_established_protocol() gap identified for D02 and D04.
trading_pattern_summary.mm_wallet_behavior: Raydium treasury MM may execute rapid round-trips within 25 slots with <1% vol diff as part of legitimate spread maintenance
trading_pattern_summary.expected_round_trips: 2-5 per 24h window for the primary MM wallet

expected_result.calibration_gap: If Signal A fires on RAY, this is an accepted FP. The detector behaves correctly per current is_established_protocol() logic (RAY does not satisfy either branch). Mitigation requires either: (a) adding RAY to an explicit suppression list, or (b) resolving the jup_verified=false gap for RAY specifically. Tagged for separate D05 P4-0 calibration task.
expected_result.action_for_developer: Run the fixture. If Signal A fires with confidence > 0.50 on RAY MM activity, set calibration_flag=true and add TODO comment linking to calibration task. Do not block the detector on this known FP.

cross_reference: Identical gap documented for D02 Signal B (4k3Dyjzv_RAY.json in solana-corpus-phase1.md, calibration_flag=true) and D04 Signal C (RAY pattern). D05 inherits the same debt.
test_assertion: calibration_flag=true means test PASSES regardless of whether Signal A fires; the fixture exists to document the FP, not to assert correctness
